Experiment Atlas
Collaboratorium — a community-powered creative laboratory
A place to experiment with new forms of collaboration, contribute to a real-world project, build a cultural library of ritual interventions, and meet others who believe that art, technology, imagination, and community can shape a more connected future.

And definitely have fun together!
🌟 Art 💡 Technology ❤ Community 🌎 Cross-cultural 🏭 Fuji Sanctuary 2026

The purpose of the Collaboratorium is to create a creative laboratory for revitalizing indigenous wisdom technologies through modern technologies and new forms of social connection.

Together, we will experiment at the intersection of art, technology, and community to prototype new forms of interactive rituals, collective experiences, and cross-cultural collaboration.

What we are building together
🏭
Interactive Rituals
New forms of participatory experience that connect people across cultures, distances, and disciplines.
📚
Cultural Library
A shared archive of experiments, prototypes, reflections, and insights that grows with each cycle.
🌎
Cross-cultural Connections
Relationships between artists, technologists, storytellers, and community builders from around the world.
Our values
Collaboration over extraction
Participation before performance. Process before products.
Experimentation over certainty
Curiosity before perfection. Listening before answers.
Relationships through reciprocity
Community built on mutual care, support, and generosity.
Participant journey Calendar Detailed structure
Collaboratorium — participant journey
Co-create · Experiment · Learn · Share · Repeat
Programme formats
Orientation
Start here. Get the vision, structure and first materials.
Experiment cycle
7-day cycle: Design → Prototype → Playtest → Reflect → Share
Collective event
All groups come together to share and learn.
Final showcase
Celebrate and present the best experiments.
Closing
Gratitude, reflection and closing the journey together.

Cycle 1 · July 14–20
Dates: July 14–20. Recommended playtest window: Sun Jul 19–Mon Jul 20 with your Radiant Iris host.
Jul 26 or Aug 2 (TBD)
Collective playtest
All groups share their playtests, see what others are building, and find new collaborators.
Aug 16 or 23 (TBD)
Final showcase
A celebration of the best prototypes and experiments. All participants and community invited.
After showcase (TBD)
Celebration, gratitude & reflection
Live closing session — honour the journey, thank each other, reflect on what we built.
Exact dates will be announced as the programme unfolds.

Groups are intentionally cross-disciplinary — we blend artists, technologists, and community builders so each team can approach the experiment from multiple angles. Your Radiant Iris hosts are there to support and guide.
My avatar
✨
It’s your magic gift!
Based on your intro video and answers, we’ll create a personal animal guide and fable just for you.
Your animal guide
The Moth Who Rewrites the Same Map
A luna moth emerges already running out of time, carrying no mouth, living on what it gathered before it became itself. It has circled the same pale branch in the same forest dark, convinced that one more orbit will resolve what the last orbit almost revealed.
Your animal guide will appear here after orientation. Each animal carries a unique gift for the group.
Your personal fable
A short story written just for you — your role in the Mirrorwood narrative, your gifts and challenges.
A short story written just for you — your role in the Mirrorwood narrative, your gifts and challenges.
Your avatar is generated from your intro video, your answers to the three questions, and what we notice about how you show up. It’s a gift from us — a mirror to help you see yourself in the story.

Look what we built together! Wow.
Every experiment here was created in 7 days by a cross-disciplinary team of strangers who became collaborators. This is what happens when art, technology, and community collide — and you give people just enough time to surprise themselves.
